title: feat: add Avian as a model provider
body:
## Summary

- Add [Avian](https://avian.io/) as a supported model provider for chat completions
- Register `avian/chat` kind in the HTTP API chat bindings alongside existing OpenAI-compatible providers
- Add documentation page with configuration examples and available models

## Details

Avian is an inference API provider with an OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` endpoint. This PR adds first-class support so users can configure it with `kind = "avian/chat"` instead of using the generic `openai/chat` kind.

**Available models:**
| Model | Context | Max Output |
|---|---|---|
| `deepseek/deepseek-v3.2` | 164K | 65K |
| `moonshotai/kimi-k2.5` | 131K | 8K |
| `z-ai/glm-5` | 131K | 16K |
| `minimax/minimax-m2.5` | 1M | 1M |

**Example configuration:**
```toml
[model.chat.http]
kind = "avian/chat"
model_name = "deepseek/deepseek-v3.2"
api_endpoint = "https://api.avian.io/v1"
api_key = "your-api-key"
```

## Changes

- `crates/http-api-bindings/src/chat/mod.rs` — Add `"avian/chat"` to the match arm for OpenAI-compatible chat providers
- `website/docs/references/models-http-api/avian.md` — Documentation page with setup instructions

## Test plan

- [x] Verified the code change follows the exact same pattern as existing providers (OpenRouter, Fireworks, DeepSeek, etc.)
- [ ] The `avian/chat` kind routes through the same `ExtendedOpenAIConfig` path as `openai/chat`
- [ ] Configuration can be tested with a valid Avian API key

cc @wsxiaoys @icycodes
author: avianion

and MiniMax M2.5.

- Add documentation page with configuration examples

title: feat(core): Add a Generic/General OAuth client
body: This PR contains changes in order to add a generalised OAuth client. Please note that I am aware this feature is behind a pay-wall and I absolutely respect the Tabby team's decision to keep this behind a pay-wall. I want to emphasise this is a POC and my intent here was to quickly learn Rust and to give back to Tabby. The benefit of having a generalised OAuth client is that end users can integrate with 3rd party identity providers (such as Auth0, Okta, Authentik, etc.). This is achieved by using [OpenID Connect Discovery](https://swagger.io/docs/specification/v3_0/authentication/openid-connect-discovery/) which contains the endpoints required to: - generate and start an authorization code flow - validate and get access tokens - grab user info On the backend, I am introducing a new migration to add a `config_url` to the `oauth_credential` table. This column is a `varchar(256)` and should accomodate most identity provider's Connect Discovery endpoints, I also changed the frontend here and here screenshots and explanations as to how the UX looks like: Existing OAuth Integrations will not display the new Config URL text field New Radio Button to show the General OAuth Configs: View the show page for a created General OAuth Configs: SSO Sign In Page: Please also note this branch contains changes from https://github.com/TabbyML/tabby/pull/4323 and I am hoping to merge the upstream PR before merging this one in.

title: feat(model): Expose the Flash Attention LLAMA flag as an environment variable
body:
This PR contains changes to expose the embedded Llama.cpp flash attention flag as an environment variable. The intent behind this PR is to expose fine grain control of how models are loaded and run. 

I am proposing to expose this via a new environment variable called `LLAMA_CPP_FAST_ATTENTION`. The intended behaviour is as follows:

- if not set, then the `fa` flag is not enabled
- if the `LLAMA_CPP_FAST_ATTENTION` is set to any value, then the `fa` flag is enabled

title: fix(http): fix repeated content-type in http headers
body:
## Summary
Fixes #4089 - Removes duplicate "content-type" header in HTTP requests to embedding API endpoints.

## Problem
When sending requests to model API endpoints, the "content-type" header was being set twice:
1. Automatically by reqwest's `.json()` method 
2. Explicitly via `.header("content-type", "application/json")`

## Solution
Removed the explicit `.header("content-type", "application/json")` call from the request builder in `/crates/http-api-bindings/src/embedding/openai.rs`.

## Testing
- Manually verified HTTP request headers no longer contain duplicate content-type entries
author: petarjuki7

title: feat(completion): implement next edit suggestion mode with edit histo…
body:
This pull request introduces a new "next edit suggestion" (NES) mode for the code completion service, along with various supporting changes to the codebase. The NES mode predicts the next edit a user might make, leveraging edit history and other contextual information. Key changes include updates to the `CompletionService` to handle NES mode, modifications to the data structures to support edit history, and logging improvements for debugging.

### Enhancements for "Next Edit Suggestion" Mode:

* **NES Mode Handling in `CompletionService`:**
  - Added a `generate_next_edit_suggestion` method to handle NES-specific logic, including building prompts from edit history and generating completions with adjusted options.
  - Updated `CompletionService` to check for NES mode and route requests accordingly.

* **Edit History Support:**
  - Introduced `EditHistory` and `CurrentVersion` structs to represent the user's edit history and current code state. These are required for NES mode. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-57b301483113a0c93d6eaf6a57792896a5c70f120f44b9f4946e80d125bc62b3R61-R88) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-57b301483113a0c93d6eaf6a57792896a5c70f120f44b9f4946e80d125bc62b3R187-R189)
  - Updated `Segments` to include an optional `edit_history` field for NES mode.

### Data Structure and Configuration Updates:

* **Mode Field Addition:**
  - Added a `mode` field to `CompletionRequest`, `CompletionOptions`, and `CodeGenerationOptions` to distinguish between "standard" and "next_edit_suggestion" modes. Default value is "standard." [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-57b301483113a0c93d6eaf6a57792896a5c70f120f44b9f4946e80d125bc62b3R61-R88) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c8c6afb2d30080942c4a2287dd509e3724a3654ead10a6458870bdeacdd373f8R28-R30) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-cbeb34121ff23f7b25a34d50d257f4e60e226c2e431b3475e4e3deee4c916debR15-R17)

* **Prompt Builders:**
  - Added a `NextEditPromptBuilder` to generate prompts specific to NES mode. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-57b301483113a0c93d6eaf6a57792896a5c70f120f44b9f4946e80d125bc62b3R289) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-57b301483113a0c93d6eaf6a57792896a5c70f120f44b9f4946e80d125bc62b3R307)

### Logging and Debugging Improvements:

* Added detailed logging for request bodies, responses, and errors in both streaming and non-streaming modes to aid debugging. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-42dc0c5e3abf318171ee5d0dbb443c598eaeb1a07cd1d5191f6e70e7e3ef7795L48-R70) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-42dc0c5e3abf318171ee5d0dbb443c598eaeb1a07cd1d5191f6e70e7e3ef7795R92-R148)

### Testing and Miscellaneous Updates:

* Updated tests to include the new `edit_history` field and ensure compatibility with NES mode. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-57b301483113a0c93d6eaf6a57792896a5c70f120f44b9f4946e80d125bc62b3R629) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-4888459614f0d61297d851e6237d8be875c6870a360670962cfdbc282215d150R296)
* Minor regex simplification in `override_generated_text`.

title: fix(index): correctly resolve file:// URLs on Windows and Unix
body:
This PR addresses inconsistencies in resolving local Git repository URLs with file:// scheme, specifically for Windows environments.

Changes Made:
Adjusted resolve_dir logic to handle file:///C:/... URLs properly using url::Url::to_file_path() and added fallback logging for invalid or unconvertible paths.

Added test cases in test_resolve_dir_handles_various_file_urls to cover:

Standard file:///C:/... paths

Lowercase drive letter formats

Encoded characters in path (%20)

Non-standard but accepted forms like file://C:/...

Added logging to debug resolve_dir results and identify mis-parsed paths.

Verified:
Ran local SchedulerGitRepository job with file:///C:/path/to/project

Confirmed successful resolution to C:\\path\\to\\project

Related Issues:
Fixes potential root cause of [#4104](https://github.com/TabbyML/tabby/issues/4104) on Windows

body:

author: liangfung

reviews.body:
## Pull Request Overview

This PR introduces a new REST API endpoint to fetch job logs and updates the job logs retrieval mechanism across the backend and frontend. Key changes include:
- Removing in-service file reading of job logs and replacing it with a dedicated log file path getter.
- Adding a new `/background-jobs/:id/logs` endpoint with proper file streaming.
- Updating the UI to use SWR for fetching and displaying job logs.
- Updating Cargo.toml dependencies to integrate the workspace version of tokio-util.

### Reviewed Changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| ee/tabby-webserver/src/service/job.rs | Migrates log handling from direct file reading to returning log file paths. |
| ee/tabby-webserver/src/routes/mod.rs | Introduces the new logs endpoint and updates related routing. |
